page: Amazon Web Services c8id.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server features 16 virtual CPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated processor operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and is supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled as a 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, offering fast local read and write capabilities. Network performance is anchored by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8id.4xlarge demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, achieving top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core scoring, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. It also excels in lightweight language model inference, reaching top-tier speeds of 390.86 tokens per second for text generation with a 135M parameter model. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations remain within the average tier.

Given its high clock speed, dedicated CPU allocation, and fast local NVMe storage, the c8id.4xlarge is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads that require rapid local data access. These workloads include high-performance web serving, software compilation, text processing, and localized database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz and a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. The architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX). Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded compression, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The server provides a balanced, high-frequency compute profile with fast local storage, making it highly efficient for workloads requiring rapid single-thread execution and low-latency disk access. It is well-suited for software compilation, text processing, web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for compute-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD for high-performance workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring high-frequency Intel processors, DDR5 memory, and fast local NVMe storage for demanding single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and database operations
